body.title: CLOSED: Retire db_proxy SQLite-compat translation layer — Phase A inventory complete
body.result: Phase A complete: callsite inventory at lab/design_docs/db-proxy-sqlite-callsites-20260505.md — 6 files enumerated, all patterns mapped, Phase B/C children to follow
body.description:
db_proxy.py still contains a SQLite-compat translation layer (_PGConnWrapper, _PGRowProxy, _translate_insert_or_replace, _INSTR_RE, executescript, PRAGMA no-op, lastrowid via LASTVAL, blanket ?->%s replace). Post-D126 all live callers run Postgres; the SQLite fallback is the dead branch. D-sqlite-removal-2026-04-22 approved the direction. The proxy layer itself (connection pool, slow-query metrics, search_path management) stays. This is an INVESTIGATE ticket: Phase A = grep all SQLite-ism callsites (?-placeholder, INSERT OR REPLACE, PRAGMA, INSTR, executescript, lastrowid) across the codebase. Phase B/C (implementation + deletion) follow after Phase A firms up the size.

**Affected files:** lab/utility_closet/db_proxy.py (SQLite shim classes), wild_igor/igor/memory/cortex.py (uses executescript for schema init), all callsites found in Phase A
**Design rules:** no-sqlite
**Scope boundary:** IN: Phase A grep + callsite map; identify all SQLite-ism usage. OUT: Phase B/C implementation deferred to child tickets after Phase A.
**Test plan:** Phase A produces a callsite inventory doc. tests/test_d126_postgres.py pins some shim behavior — note which tests need updating in Phase B.
**Pass condition:** A callsite inventory doc exists naming every file using SQLite-isms, with proposed Postgres equivalents for each.
